text: The surgeon opens the retroperitoneum and identifies the ureter, performing gentle dissection to locate the anterior division of the internal iliac artery. The assistant retracts the uterus manually, allowing the surgeon to apply uterine clips on the anterior division, with the ureter visibly distant from the clipping site.

The surgeon performs dissection on the opposite side, with the assistant retracting the uterus. The uterine artery is isolated and dissected, with the ureter visibly distant from the uterine artery. Bilateral uterine artery clipping is performed under direct vision.

Following bilateral uterine artery clipping, the uterus appears white due to reduced vascularity. The surgeon begins the myomectomy by making incisions on the most prominent aspects of the fibroids using a laparoscopic shearer.

The surgeon removes the first fibroid followed by a large posterior fibroid, with minimal bleeding observed at the fibroid base due to bilateral uterine artery clipping. The large fibroid base is visible with negligible bleeding. After enucleating all fibroids, uterine reconstruction is performed using quill sutures.
The surgeon completes the procedure by removing the fibroids through morcellation, demonstrating the effectiveness of the technique in managing multiple large uterine fibroids.
